title: Divide by 1
description: Divide by 1.  Quotients are less than or equal to 10.

hints:
We can think of $\blueD{1} \div \greenD{1}$ as putting $\blueD1$ circle into  $\greenD1$ group:

[[☃ image 1]]

How many circles are in the group?
$\Large\blueD{1} \div \greenD{1} = {1}$

We can think of $\blueD{2} \div \greenD{1}$ as putting $\blueD2$ circles into  $\greenD1$ group:

[[☃ image 1]]

How many circles are in the group?

There are $\maroonD2$ circles in the group.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\Large\blueD{2} \div \greenD{1} = \maroonD{2}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can think of $\blueD{2} \div \greenD{1}$ as dividing $\blueD2$ circles into  groups of  $\greenD1$:

[[☃ image 1]]

How many groups are there?

There are $\maroonD2$ groups of $\greenD1$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\Large\blueD{2} \div \greenD{1} = \maroonD{2}$

[[☃ explanation 1]]

We can think of $\blueD{3} \div \greenD{1}$ as putting $\blueD3$ circles into  $\greenD1$ group:

[[☃ image 1]]

How many circles are in the group?
$\Large\blueD{3} \div \greenD{1} = {3}$

We can think of $\blueD{4} \div \greenD{1}$ as putting $\blueD4$ circles into  $\greenD1$ group:

[[☃ image 1]]

$\Large\blueD{4} \div \greenD{1} = {4}$

We can think of $\blueD{5} \div \greenD{1}$ as putting $\blueD5$ circles into  $\greenD1$ group:

[[☃ image 1]]
